text:
Everybody knows how the opening ceremony at the Paris 2024 Olympics contained a depiction of The Last Supper in mockery of millions of watching Christians. What is not known at all is the disgraceful action of the French government when a small group of Christians decided to stage a protest. They hired a bus bearing the slogan “stop attacks on Christians”. There was of course nothing unlawful about such an activity and the bus was driven around Paris attracting many waves and signs of agreement. Naturally there was a large police presence in the city centre and the bus drove past hundreds of French police with no intervention at all.

This carried on all morning but later that afternoon the bus was flagged down at gunpoint and armed police surrounded the vehicle! The occupants were arrested for “organising a demonstration” without the approval of the French government.

That was rot as they had already researched the law and definitively established that such regulations applied to large scale marches not a slogan on a bus. Yet they were handcuffed, taken to a secure facility and had all their belongings confiscated. They were then strip searched and refused access to lawyers.

Kept in custody overnight, some of them were refused food and water (welcome to Third World France) and also the ability to contact families (welcome to the French Stasi). The next day they were finally released after prosecutors ruled what everybody had known all along: no offence had been committed.

Sources have suggested that the intervention was political and it does seem the only plausible explanation, but what worries me is the way this outrage has been ignored by the entire British press and media. I know for a fact that one high circulation Sunday paper had all the details but decided the story was not worth even a few lines.

Well, naturally!

After all, it was only a bunch of devout Christians, so who cares? What right have they to free speech? Well, they are probably suing, so we may get some answers to those questions.

title: The disgraceful French clampdown on a legal protest | Ann Widdecombe | Columnists | Comment | Express.co.uk
author: Ann Widdecombe
description: The Paris 2024 Olympics contained a depiction of The Last Supper in mockery of millions of watching Christians.
